html:
Fact Symbols with Injective Instances


None


Multiset Rewriting Rules


rule (modulo AC) isend:
   [ !KU( x ) ] --[ K( x ) ]-> [ In( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo AC) irecv:
   [ Out( x ) ] --> [ !KD( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */


rule (modulo E) Generate_Signing_Keypair:
   [ Fr( ~sk ) ]
  -->
   [ !SignKey( $A, ~sk ), !VerifyKey( $A, pk(~sk) ), Out( pk(~sk) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) RevealSign_Message:
   [ In( m ), !SignKey( A, sk ) ]
  --[ RevealSigned( A, m ) ]->
   [ Out( revealSign(m, sk) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Extract_From_RevealSign:
   [ In( rs ) ]
  --[ MessageExtracted( getMessage(rs) ) ]->
   [ Out( getMessage(rs) ) ]

  /*
  rule (modulo AC) Extract_From_RevealSign:
     [ In( rs ) ] --[ MessageExtracted( z ) ]-> [ Out( z ) ]
    variants (modulo AC)
    1. rs = rs.3
       z = getMessage(rs.3)
    
    2. rs = revealSign(x.3, x.4)
       z = x.3
  */
rule (modulo E) Verify_RevealSignature:
   [ In( rs ), In( m ), !VerifyKey( A, pka ) ]
  --[ RevealSigVerified( A, m ) ]->
   [ Out( revealVerify(rs, m, pka) ) ]

  /*
  rule (modulo AC) Verify_RevealSignature:
     [ In( rs ), In( m ), !VerifyKey( A, pka ) ]
    --[ RevealSigVerified( A, m ) ]->
     [ Out( z ) ]
    variants (modulo AC)
    1. m = m.6
       pka = pka.6
       rs = rs.6
       z = revealVerify(rs.6, m.6, pka.6)
    
    2. m = x.6
       pka = pk(x.7)
       rs = revealSign(x.6, x.7)
       z = true
  */
rule (modulo E) Invalid_Extract_Key_From_RevealSign:
   [ In( revealSign(m, sk) ) ] --> [ Out( sk ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Invalid_Forge_RevealSignature:
   [ In( m ), In( pk(sk) ) ] --> [ Out( revealSign(m, sk) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Protocol_RevealSign_Send:
   [ Fr( ~m ), !SignKey( $A, ~skA ) ]
  --[ ProtocolRevealSigned( $A, $B, ~m ) ]->
   [
   Out( <$A, revealSign(~m, ~skA)> ),
   StoreRevealMsg( $A, $B, ~m )
   ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) Protocol_Extract_Verify:
   [ In( <A, sig> ), !VerifyKey( A, pk ) ]
  --[ ProtocolRevealVerified( A, $B ) ]->
   [
   RevealVerifiedMsg( $B, A, getMessage(sig),
                      revealVerify(sig, getMessage(sig), pk)
   )
   ]

  /*
  rule (modulo AC) Protocol_Extract_Verify:
     [ In( <A, sig> ), !VerifyKey( A, pk ) ]
    --[ ProtocolRevealVerified( A, $B ) ]->
     [ RevealVerifiedMsg( $B, A, z, z.1 ) ]
    variants (modulo AC)
    1. pk = pk.7
       sig = sig.7
       z = getMessage(sig.7)
       z.1 = revealVerify(sig.7, getMessage(sig.7), pk.7)
    
    2. pk = pk.9
       sig = revealSign(x.7, x.8)
       z = x.7
       z.1 = revealVerify(revealSign(x.7, x.8), x.7, pk.9)
    
    3. pk = pk(x.7)
       sig = revealSign(x.8, x.7)
       z = x.8
       z.1 = true
  */
rule (modulo E) Reveal_Sign_Key:
   [ !SignKey( A, sk ) ] --[ KeyReveal( A ) ]-> [ Out( sk ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
rule (modulo E) RevealSign_With_Compromised_Key:
   [ In( sk ), In( m ) ]
  --[ CompromisedRevealSign( m ) ]->
   [ Out( revealSign(m, sk) ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */
title: Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions
